title: BUG: Add delimiters to NameObject.renumber_table
body:
Per the spec, name objects cannot contain unescaped delimiters, which include angle, curly and square brackets (currently missing).

I've attached a sample input PDF with a valid, correctly escaped name object. When appending this to a new PdfWriter the output is invalid (incorrectly escaped):

```
>>> w = PdfWriter()
>>> w.append('nameobj.pdf')
>>> w.write('out.pdf')
```
[out.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20173760/out.pdf)
[nameobj.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20173761/nameobj.pdf)

step_plan.summary: Fix PDF NameObject serialization so all delimiter characters forbidden unescaped in PDF names (notably <, >, [, ], {, }) are escaped as #XX during writing. Add regression tests to ensure PdfWriter output remains valid when rewriting PDFs containing correctly escaped name objects.

steps.operation: Update NameObject escaping table usage in renumber to include PDF delimiter characters and ensure it applies to all bytes produced for the name
steps.reason: The writer path serializes names via NameObject.write_to_stream() → NameObject.renumber(); the bug is that some delimiter characters (<, >, [, ], {, }) are not being escaped as #XX, producing invalid PDF names per spec when rewriting/cloning objects.
steps.side_effects: Will change serialized output bytes for names containing these delimiters (now spec-compliant). Downstream tests that assert exact output may need updating. Should not affect parsing because #XX decoding already exists.

steps.operation: Verify NameObject serialization always goes through renumber and (if needed) adjust write_to_stream to consistently serialize the renumbered/escaped representation
steps.reason: write_to_stream is the central serialization entry point used by PdfWriter; confirming it always uses renumber() guarantees the updated escaping is applied in all writer scenarios (append/merge/clone/write).
steps.side_effects: If any alternate serialization path is corrected to route through renumber(), more names may become escaped than before (intended). Could affect byte-for-byte golden tests and external consumers that relied on the previous non-compliant output.

steps.operation: Add/extend regression assertions for delimiter escaping in PDF name objects, including angle/square/curly brackets serialization as #3C/#3E/#5B/#5D/#7B/#7D
steps.reason: Prevents regressions by ensuring NameObject.write_to_stream produces spec-compliant escapes for all delimiter characters. This directly covers the reported failure mode when rewriting PDFs with valid escaped names.
steps.side_effects: Test expectations may need to be updated to reflect new escaping behavior. May require adding explicit byte-string expectations or parametrized cases to keep the test readable and stable across platforms/encodings.
